reason: Black Ghost Knifefish are known jumpers that require a tight-fitting lid; a typed escapeRisk enum (low/moderate/high) would help surface this to fishkeepers and is already used in the crustacean variant.

Blood Parrot Cichlid and other hybrid species (e.g. Flowerhorn) have no formal scientific name and unique husbandry implications; a typed boolean would let the UI flag hybrid origin and explain the null-ish scientific name pattern.

Lobelia cardinalis thrives at unusually low temperatures (down to 16 °C) compared to most aquarium plants; a typed boolean flag would allow the UI to surface cold-water or unheated-tank suitability to fishkeepers.

Coral husbandry sources consistently specify nitrate (and often phosphate) target ranges separate from pH/kH/salinity; a typed nullableNumericRange field would make reef water-quality guidance machine-readable rather than buried in careNotes.

Several aquatic stem plants (e.g. Creeping Jenny, Bacopa, Hygrophila) can grow fully emersed and are sold/used in paludariums — a boolean flag would help filter species suitable for riparian or above-waterline planting.
Bacopa monnieri readily grows emersed and is widely used in paludariums and pond margins; a typed boolean would let the UI surface species suitable for above-waterline or riparian planting.

Acropora and many other SPS/LPS corals are routinely propagated by fragging (cutting) in captivity; a typed boolean would let the UI surface hobbyist-propagation suitability and is a commonly sought trait in the reef community.

Acropora care is particularly sensitive to nitrate levels (1–10 ppm target); a typed nullableNumericRange for nitrate (and phosphate) would make reef water-quality guidance machine-readable rather than buried in careNotes.

African Butterflyfish can jump several metres out of water and absolutely require a tight-fitting lid; a typed escapeRisk enum (low/moderate/high) on the fish variant — parallel to the crustacean variant — would surface this critical safety requirement to fishkeepers in a machine-readable way.
Goniopora care sources specify a nitrate tolerance range (1–20 ppm); a typed nullableNumericRange for nitrate would make reef water-quality guidance machine-readable rather than buried in careNotes.

Goniopora is routinely aquaculture-fragged (ORA strains are significantly hardier than wild-caught); a typed boolean on the coral variant would surface propagation suitability and support captive-bred sourcing guidance.

Several dwarf angelfish and wrasses form harems in the wild (and should be kept as mated pairs or harems in captivity); a typed boolean on the fish variant would allow the UI to surface harem-keeping guidance and protogynous hermaphroditism warnings to fishkeepers.

French Angelfish juveniles serve as cleaners at cleaning stations (removing parasites from other fish), a behaviorally significant trait relevant to tank setup and fish interactions; a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface this unique ecological role to fishkeepers.

Seahorses and pipefish require solid anchor points (hitching posts) to rest and reduce stress — a typed boolean on the fish variant would let the UI surface this non-obvious tank-setup requirement distinct from general decorPreferences.

Foxface Lo and other rabbitfish (Siganidae) have venomous dorsal and anal spines that can inflict painful stings; a typed boolean on the fish variant would let the UI surface a safety warning to aquarists and is a husbandry-critical trait distinct from aggression.

Clownfish and several other marine fish species are protandrous (male-first) sequential hermaphrodites; a typed boolean on the fish variant would let the UI surface sex-change behavior warnings and pair-acquisition guidance to fishkeepers.

Rhinecanthus rectangulus and several other triggerfish species are noted for audible grunting/vocalizations, a distinctive husbandry fact that affects keeper experience; a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface species capable of vocalization in the UI.

Synchiropus splendidus (and other scaleless/mucus-covered dragonets) are highly sensitive to copper-based treatments — a typed boolean on the fish variant, parallel to the crustacean variant's copperSensitive field, would surface this critical medication safety warning to reef keepers.

Paternal mouthbrooding is a significant reproductive strategy in cardinalfish (Apogonidae) and other marine species that affects breeding difficulty, fry survival, and captive husbandry decisions; a typed boolean would make this trait machine-readable and surfaceable in breeding filter UIs.

Lightning Maroon Clownfish and similar captive-bred designer morphs (e.g. Picasso, Wyoming White) have no typed flag distinguishing them from wild-type variants; a boolean on the fish variant would let the UI surface morph-origin info and help buyers understand captive-bred vs wild-type availability.

Surgeonfish (Acanthuridae) possess a sharp caudal-peduncle spine (scalpel) that can inflict painful wounds; a typed boolean on the fish variant — distinct from venomousSpines — would let the UI surface handling-safety warnings specific to tangs and surgeonfish.

Yellow Tang and several other tang/surgeonfish species are notably prone to HLLE (Head and Lateral Line Erosion); a typed boolean on the fish variant would let the UI surface disease-risk warnings and diet/water-quality guidance specific to HLLE-susceptible species.

Rhinomuraena quaesita exists in three color phases (black juvenile, blue adult male, yellow female) that are sold and searched under different common names (Black/Blue/Yellow Ribbon Eel) but are the same species; a typed field referencing the canonical species slug would enable deduplication UI logic and cross-linking between phase entries.

Ribbon Eels are notorious escape artists that require a weighted, fully-sealed lid; a typed escapeRisk enum (low/moderate/high) on the fish variant — parallel to the crustacean variant — would surface this critical safety requirement to aquarists in a machine-readable way.

Clownfish (and several other marine fish) are protandrous sequential hermaphrodites; all start male and the dominant individual becomes female. A typed boolean on the fish variant would surface sex-change behavior and pair-acquisition guidance — second independent proposal after sw-fish-001.

The Black Percula / Darwin Clownfish is a naturally occurring geographic color morph of A. ocellaris (not a captive designer morph); a typed boolean distinguishing natural morphs from designer/selectively-bred variants would help the UI and buyers understand the fish's origin and conservation context.

Horn Sharks and several other elasmobranchs (e.g. Port Jackson shark, spiny dogfish) possess paired dorsal fin spines used defensively; a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface a handling-safety warning to aquarists distinct from general venom or aggression fields.

Blue Dot Stingray (and other elasmobranch rays) possess a venomous tail spine used defensively — a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface a safety warning to aquarists distinct from aggression, parallel to the proposal already made for sw-fish-007 (Foxface Lo).

Elasmobranchs (sharks and rays) are highly sensitive to copper-based medications; a typed boolean on the fish variant — parallel to the crustacean variant's copperSensitive field — would surface this critical medication safety warning for rays, sharks, and other copper-intolerant fish. Second independent proposal after sw-fish-010 (Green Mandarin).

Meiacanthus atrodorsalis possesses venomous canine teeth used defensively; painful bites from finger-feeding are documented — a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface this safety warning to aquarists, distinct from aggression. Third independent proposal (after sw-fish-007 and sw-fish-019).

Forktail Blenny is a documented jumper requiring a tight-fitting lid; a typed escapeRisk enum (low/moderate/high) on the fish variant — parallel to the crustacean variant — would surface this critical safety requirement. Third independent proposal (after fw-fish-002 and sw-fish-017).

Dwarf Seahorse and other extremely slow/fragile fish require species-only aquariums to avoid starvation; a typed boolean (analogous to crustacean.speciesOnlyTankRecommended) would surface this critical compatibility constraint to fishkeepers in the UI.

All Acanthurus surgeonfish possess a sharp, blade-like caudal-peduncle spine (scalpel) that can inflict painful wounds on handlers and tankmates; a typed boolean on the fish variant — distinct from venomousSpines — would surface a handling-safety warning specific to tangs and surgeonfish. Second independent proposal after sw-fish-018 (Blue Caribbean Tang).

Glass Eye Squirrelfish and other squirrelfish/bigeye family fish are strictly nocturnal, hiding during daylight and becoming active at dusk — a typed boolean on the fish variant would let the UI surface lighting-schedule and feeding-time guidance for nocturnal species.

Firefish (and dartfish generally) are well-documented jumpers that require an escape-proof lid; a typed escapeRisk enum (low/moderate/high) on the fish variant — parallel to the crustacean variant — would surface this critical safety requirement to saltwater aquarists in a machine-readable way.

Greenbanded Goby and several other marine gobies are protogynous hermaphrodites (female-first sex changers); a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface this sex-change behavior to fishkeepers and is distinct from the existing protandrous proposal covering clownfish.

Gray Angelfish juveniles (and French Angelfish juveniles) operate as cleaners at cleaning stations, removing ectoparasites from other fish — a behaviorally significant trait relevant to tank planning and fish interactions; a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface this ecological role to fishkeepers. Second independent proposal after sw-fish-008 (French Angelfish).

Garden Eels are known to jump from open tanks and require a fully sealed lid; a typed escapeRisk enum (low/moderate/high) on the fish variant — parallel to the crustacean variant — would surface this safety requirement. Fifth independent proposal (after fw-fish-002, sw-fish-017, sw-fish-026, sw-fish-025).

Garden Eels and other burrowing species require a specific minimum sand-bed depth (20–30 cm for this species) that cannot be captured by existing tank fields; a typed numeric field would surface this substrate requirement clearly in the UI.

Dactylopus dactylopus is a scaleless, mucus-covered dragonet (Callionymidae) that should be assumed highly sensitive to copper-based medications — a typed boolean on the fish variant, parallel to the crustacean variant copperSensitive field, would surface this critical medication safety warning. Third independent proposal after sw-fish-010 (Green Mandarin) and sw-fish-019 (Blue Dot Stingray).

Naso unicornis possesses two permanently erect non-retractable caudal-peduncle spines that can easily injure handlers and tangle in nets; all Naso and Acanthurus surgeonfish carry this trait — a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface a handling-safety warning distinct from venomousSpines. Third independent proposal (after sw-fish-018 Blue Caribbean Tang and sw-fish-023 Epaulette Surgeonfish).

Harlequin Bass (and other Serranus spp.) are synchronous hermaphrodites — each individual possesses both male and female reproductive organs simultaneously; a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface this unusual reproductive biology to aquarists and pair-keepers.

Assessor flavissimus males are paternal mouthbrooders (incubate egg masses in the mouth); a typed boolean on the fish variant would make this reproductively-significant trait machine-readable and surfaceable in breeding filter UIs — second independent proposal after sw-fish-012 (Banggai Cardinalfish).

Griffis Angelfish (Apolemichthys griffisi) is documented as hermaphroditic (protogynous) with no visible sex differentiation; a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface sex-change behavior warnings to fishkeepers, distinct from the existing protandrous proposal covering clownfish.

Some butterflyfish (e.g. Chaetodon octofasciatus, C. baronessa) are obligate corallivores that cannot be weaned onto prepared foods in captivity; a typed boolean on the fish variant would let the UI surface an extreme diet-difficulty warning distinct from careLevel and reefSafe, and help buyers identify species with very low long-term captive survival.

Harlequin Tusk and other large wrasses are documented rearrangers of substrate and rockwork in captivity; a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface a tank-setup safety warning (secure rockwork to prevent collapses) to fishkeepers in a machine-readable way.

Fuzzy Dwarf Lionfish has 13 venomous dorsal spines capable of inflicting painful stings; a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface a safety warning to aquarists distinct from aggression. Fourth independent proposal (after sw-fish-007, sw-fish-019, and sw-fish-026).

Fuzzy Dwarf Lionfish is strictly nocturnal — it hides during daylight and is active at night, which directly affects feeding schedule and tank lighting decisions. Second independent proposal after sw-fish-030 (Glass Eye Squirrelfish).

Genicanthus lamarck forms harems of one dominant male and 2–6 females in the wild, and keeping groups is advised in the hobby; a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface harem-keeping guidance and protogynous hermaphroditism context to fishkeepers. Second independent proposal after sw-fish-005.

Chaetodon semilarvatus and several other butterflyfish species form lifelong monogamous pair bonds in the wild, a trait with direct implications for how they should be acquired and housed in captivity; a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface pair-bonding guidance to aquarists.

Centropyge vrolikii forms monogamous home-ranging pairs in the wild and is a protogynous hermaphrodite; a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface harem-keeping and sex-change guidance to fishkeepers. Third independent proposal after sw-fish-005 (Flame Angelfish) and sw-fish-049 (Lamarck's Angelfish).

Emperor Red Snapper (Lutjanus sebae) and several large predatory snappers and groupers in the Indo-Pacific are known vectors for ciguatera fish poisoning; a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface a food-safety and wild-harvest warning to hobbyists researching these species.

Eques lanceolatus belongs to family Sciaenidae (drums/croakers), named for males producing audible drumming sounds via the swimbladder — a distinctive behavioral trait relevant to hobbyist experience; a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface species capable of vocalization in the UI. Second independent proposal after sw-fish-002 (Humu Rectangle Triggerfish).

Koran Angelfish (and several other Pomacanthus species) undergo a dramatic multi-year juvenile-to-adult color transformation so visually distinct that juveniles are sometimes sold under different names or not recognized as the same fish; a typed boolean on the fish variant would let the UI flag species with major ontogenetic color changes and surface guidance about the transition period.

Jewel Damselfish (and several other damsels/wrasses) undergo dramatic coloration changes from juvenile to adult; a typed boolean would allow the UI to warn buyers that the fish they see in vendor photos may look very different when mature.

Queen Angelfish juveniles are well-documented cleaners at cleaning stations — picking ectoparasites from other fish before transitioning to sponge feeding as adults; a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface this ecological role to fishkeepers. Third independent proposal after sw-fish-008 (French Angelfish) and sw-fish-035 (Gray Angelfish).

Chaetodontoplus mesoleucus is documented as hermaphroditic and forms small groups in the wild; a typed haremic boolean on the fish variant would surface harem-keeping and sex-change guidance to fishkeepers, consistent with other angelfish proposals.

Little Spinefoot (Siganus spinus) shares with Foxface Lo the same family Siganidae venomous dorsal and anal fin spines; a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface a safety warning to aquarists — second independent proposal after sw-fish-007.

Panther Grouper (Cromileptes altivelis) and many other groupers are protogynous hermaphrodites (born female, dominant individuals transition to male); a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface this sex-change biology to aquarists, matching proposals from other grouper/serranid entries.

Taenianotus triacanthus has venom glands attached to its dorsal, anal, and pelvic spines — stings are documented as painful and potentially serious; a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface a safety warning to aquarists distinct from aggression. Fifth independent proposal (after sw-fish-007, sw-fish-019, sw-fish-026, and sw-fish-028).

Japanese Dragon Eel is a notorious escape artist requiring a weighted, fully-sealed lid with no gaps; a typed escapeRisk enum (low/moderate/high) on the fish variant — parallel to the crustacean variant — would surface this critical safety requirement to saltwater aquarists in a machine-readable way. Sixth independent proposal after fw-fish-002, sw-fish-017, sw-fish-026, sw-fish-025, and sw-fish-029.

Striped Shrimpfish and other shrimpfish (Centriscidae) swim in a permanent vertical posture with snout pointing downward, a locomotion trait that affects tank setup (needs vertical swimming space) and is not captured by swimZone; a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface this unusual orientation to fishkeepers.

Royal Gramma Basslet is a well-documented jumper requiring a tightly fitted lid; a typed escapeRisk enum (low/moderate/high) on the fish variant — parallel to the crustacean variant — would surface this critical safety requirement to saltwater aquarists in a machine-readable way.

Sleeper Railway Glider Goby is documented as a jumper requiring a tight-fitting lid; a typed escapeRisk enum (low/moderate/high) on the fish variant — parallel to the crustacean variant — would surface this critical safety requirement to saltwater aquarists. Sixth independent proposal for this field.

Lyretail Anthias (Pseudanthias squamipinnis) has been documented jumping from open aquaria; a typed escapeRisk enum (low/moderate/high) on the fish variant — parallel to the crustacean variant — would surface this safety requirement to saltwater aquarists. Sixth independent proposal (after fw-fish-002, sw-fish-017, sw-fish-026, sw-fish-025, sw-fish-029).

Pomacanthus maculosus and other Pomacanthidae are protogynous hermaphrodites (female-first sex changers); this affects pair formation, breeding feasibility, and sexing guidance — a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface hermaphroditism warnings to fishkeepers. Third independent proposal after sw-fish-038 (Griffis Angelfish) and sw-fish-058 (Panther Grouper).

Pomacanthus maculosus juveniles display vivid blue-and-white vertical barring that transforms dramatically into the adult yellow-bar pattern over multiple years; this ontogenetic color change is so pronounced that juveniles are often not recognized as the same species in the trade. Second independent proposal after sw-fish-048 (Koran Angelfish).

Cowfish (Ostraciidae) and a small number of other species can release pahutoxin/ostracitoxin into the water when stressed or injured, potentially killing all tankmates including themselves — a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface this critical safety warning in the UI, distinct from venomousSpines or aggression.

Pseudanthias squamipinnis is a well-known protogynous hermaphrodite (female-first sex changer); a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface sex-change behavior and harem-acquisition guidance to aquarists. Second independent proposal after sw-fish-037 (Greenbanded Goby).

Antennarius striatus possesses a venomous third dorsal spine that can inflict intense pain (comparable to a wasp sting) in humans; a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface a handling-safety warning to aquarists, distinct from aggression. Fourth independent proposal (after sw-fish-007, sw-fish-019, and sw-fish-026).

Acanthurus sohal, like all surgeonfish (Acanthuridae), possesses a sharp blade-like caudal-peduncle spine (scalpel) that can inflict painful wounds on handlers and tank mates; a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface a handling-safety warning distinct from venomousSpines. Fourth independent proposal after sw-fish-018 (Blue Caribbean Tang), sw-fish-023 (Epaulette Surgeonfish), and sw-fish-013 (Bluespine Unicorn Tang).

Sohal Tang is documented as susceptible to Head and Lateral Line Erosion (HLLE) when water quality or dietary diversity is inadequate; a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface a disease-risk warning and diet/water-quality guidance for HLLE-prone species. Second independent proposal after sw-fish-015 (Yellow Tang Hawaii).

Elasmobranchs (sharks and rays, including Chiloscyllium punctatum) are highly sensitive to copper-based medications — a typed boolean on the fish variant, parallel to the crustacean variant copperSensitive field, would surface this critical medication safety warning; fourth independent proposal after sw-fish-010, sw-fish-019, and sw-fish-024.

Chiloscyllium punctatum is strictly nocturnal, hiding during daylight and active at night — a typed boolean on the fish variant would let the UI surface lighting-schedule and feeding-time guidance for nocturnal species; third independent proposal after sw-fish-030 (Glass Eye Squirrelfish) and sw-fish-028 (Fuzzy Dwarf Lionfish).

Lined Seahorse is strongly advised to be kept in a species-only or pipefish-only aquarium — faster-moving tankmates outcompete them for food leading to starvation; a typed boolean (analogous to crustacean.speciesOnlyTankRecommended) would surface this critical compatibility constraint. Second independent proposal after sw-fish-020 (Dwarf Seahorse).

Lined Seahorse forms monogamous pair bonds with daily greeting rituals; this affects acquisition and housing decisions; a typed boolean would surface pair-bonding guidance in the UI. Second independent proposal after sw-fish-034 (Golden Semilarvatus Butterflyfish).

Pilotfish (and a few other species like remoras) exhibit a semi-obligate commensal relationship with sharks, rays, and large pelagic animals — a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface this unusual ecological dependency to public-aquarium keepers who need to understand the behavioral implications of keeping such species in isolation.

Ingen's Seahorse and other seahorses/pipefish use their prehensile tails to anchor onto solid structures; a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface this non-obvious tank-setup requirement (gorgonians, branches, macroalgae) distinctly from general decorPreferences. Third independent proposal after sw-fish-004 (Estuary Seahorse) and sw-fish-053 (Lined Seahorse).

Ingen's Seahorse is a slow feeder that is easily outcompeted by faster-moving fish; keeping in a species-only or pipefish-only aquarium is strongly advised to prevent starvation — a typed boolean (analogous to crustacean.speciesOnlyTankRecommended) would surface this to fishkeepers. Fourth independent proposal after sw-fish-004, sw-fish-020, and sw-fish-053.

Sleeper Blue Dot Goby and other Valenciennea gobies are well-documented jumpers requiring a tight-fitting lid; a typed escapeRisk enum (low/moderate/high) on the fish variant — parallel to the crustacean variant — would surface this critical safety requirement to saltwater aquarists in a machine-readable way.

Sleeper gobies (Valenciennea spp.) and other obligate sand-sifters require a minimum sand-bed depth (7–10 cm recommended) for burrowing and feeding behavior; a typed numeric field would surface this substrate requirement clearly in the UI distinct from general decorPreferences. Second independent proposal after sw-fish-029 (Garden Eel).

Chaetodon pelewensis forms monogamous pair bonds in the wild (consistent with other Chaetodon butterflyfish); a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface pair-bonding acquisition guidance to aquarists — second independent proposal after sw-fish-034 (Golden Semilarvatus Butterflyfish).

Threadfin Lookdown grows to ~49 cm and requires a tank of 1500+ liters in a schooling group; multiple sources note it is practically only feasible in public aquariums when full-grown; a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface a practicality warning to home aquarists researching large pelagic species.

Acanthurus maculiceps, like all surgeonfish (Acanthuridae), possesses a sharp blade-like caudal-peduncle spine (scalpel) that can inflict painful wounds on handlers and tank mates; a typed boolean would surface a handling-safety warning distinct from venomousSpines. Fifth independent proposal after sw-fish-018, sw-fish-023, sw-fish-013, and sw-fish-066.

Black-Eared Tang, like other Acanthurus surgeonfish, is notably prone to HLLE (Head and Lateral Line Erosion) when water quality or dietary diversity is inadequate; a typed boolean would surface a disease-risk warning and diet/water-quality guidance for HLLE-prone species. Third independent proposal after sw-fish-015 (Yellow Tang Hawaii) and sw-fish-066 (Sohal Tang).

Arothron meleagris and other pufferfish contain tetrodotoxin in their organs and skin; severely stressed specimens may release toxins that can harm or kill tankmates — a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface this safety warning distinct from venomousSpines or aggression. Second independent proposal after sw-fish-055 (Longhorn Cowfish).

Blackmouth Bicolor Chromis undergoes a dramatic coloration change from vivid yellow-and-black juveniles to dull brown-and-black adults; a typed boolean would allow the UI to warn buyers that the fish sold under this name looks very different when mature. Second independent proposal for this field after sw-fish-047 (Jewel Damselfish).

Antennata Lionfish is strictly nocturnal — it hides in crevices during daylight and hunts at night, which directly affects feeding schedule and tank lighting decisions; a typed boolean on the fish variant would let the UI surface lighting-schedule and feeding-time guidance for nocturnal species. Third independent proposal after sw-fish-030 (Glass Eye Squirrelfish) and sw-fish-028 (Fuzzy Dwarf Lionfish).

Zebra Moray (Gymnomuraena zebra) is documented as often active during daytime hours, unlike most moray species which are strictly nocturnal — a typed boolean on the fish variant would let the UI surface day/night activity patterns and help keepers plan feeding schedules; fourth independent proposal after sw-fish-030 (Glass Eye Squirrelfish), sw-fish-028 (Fuzzy Dwarf Lionfish), and sw-fish-057 (Marbled Bamboo Cat Shark).

Antennata Lionfish is strictly nocturnal — it hides in crevices during daylight and hunts at night, which directly affects feeding schedule and tank lighting decisions; a typed boolean on the fish variant would let the UI surface lighting-schedule and feeding-time guidance for nocturnal species. Fifth independent proposal after sw-fish-030, sw-fish-028, sw-fish-057, and sw-096.

Amphiprion polymnus (and all clownfish) are protandrous sequential hermaphrodites — all individuals born male, dominant becomes female — which directly affects pair acquisition and conspecific housing guidance; a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface this sex-change behavior to fishkeepers. Third independent proposal after sw-fish-001 (Gold Stripe Maroon Clownfish) and sw-fish-016 (Black Percula Clownfish).

Cephalopholis argus (Blue Dot Grouper) is documented as an invasive species in Hawaii, where it was introduced and has had significant ecological impacts on native reef fish populations; a typed array of region strings would let the UI flag ecologically sensitive species and inform hobbyists of invasive status concerns.

Pomacanthus navarchus juveniles display vivid blue-and-white patterning dramatically different from the adult blue-girdle coloration; the transformation takes multiple years and juveniles are sometimes not recognized as the same species in the trade — a typed boolean on the fish variant would let the UI flag species with major ontogenetic color changes and surface guidance about the transition period. Third independent proposal after sw-fish-048 (Koran Angelfish) and sw-fish-056 (Maculosus Angelfish).

Blueline Angelfish juveniles display bold blue-and-white vertical stripes while adults develop horizontal electric-blue lines on a mustard body — the transformation is visually so dramatic that juveniles are often not recognized as the same species in the trade; a typed boolean would let the UI flag species with major ontogenetic color changes and surface guidance about the transition period. Third independent proposal after sw-fish-048 (Koran Angelfish) and sw-fish-056 (Maculosus Angelfish).

Centropyge multispinis forms small harems in the wild (one dominant male, several females); a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface harem-keeping and protogynous hermaphroditism guidance to fishkeepers — fourth independent proposal after sw-fish-005 (Flame), sw-fish-049 (Lamarck), and sw-fish-039 (Half-Black Angelfish).

Hemiscyllium ocellatum is an elasmobranch (cartilaginous fish) and, like all sharks and rays, is highly sensitive to copper-based medications — a typed boolean on the fish variant, parallel to the crustacean variant copperSensitive field, would surface this critical medication safety warning; fifth independent proposal after sw-fish-010, sw-fish-019, sw-fish-024, and sw-fish-057.

Gymnothorax melatremus and other moray eels are frequently noted to be bite hazards due to powerful jaws and sharp teeth combined with poor eyesight; a typed boolean would surface a handler-safety warning distinct from venomousSpines and separate from conspecificAggression (which is about tankmate behavior, not human handling).

Centropyge nox forms harems of 3–7 individuals (one dominant male, remaining females) in the wild; a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface harem-keeping and pair-acquisition guidance to fishkeepers, consistent with multiple prior proposals for other Centropyge species (sw-fish-005, sw-fish-049, sw-fish-039, sw-fish-093).

Some fish species (e.g. Chrysiptera springeri, certain wrasses) are noted for consuming specific nuisance pests (Planaria flatworms, Aiptasia, etc.) — a typed nullable string field would let the UI surface pest-control utility without burying it in careNotes.

Blue Velvet Damselfish and other damsels/wrasses undergo dramatic coloration changes from juvenile to adult (electric-blue striped to uniform black/brown); a typed boolean would allow the UI to warn buyers that the fish they purchase may look very different when mature — third independent proposal after sw-fish-047 (Jewel Damselfish) and sw-fish-078 (Blackmouth Bicolor Chromis).

Lutjanus kasmira (Bluestripe Snapper) and other Lutjanidae in the Indo-Pacific are documented ciguatera fish poisoning vectors; a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface a food-safety and wild-harvest warning to hobbyists researching large predatory snappers. Second independent proposal after sw-fish-022 (Emperor Red Snapper).

Dragonface Pipefish, like all Syngnathidae, use their prehensile tails to anchor onto gorgonians, coral branches, and macroalgae; a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface this non-obvious tank-setup requirement to fishkeepers distinctly from general decorPreferences. Fourth independent proposal after sw-fish-004 (Estuary Seahorse), sw-fish-053 (Lined Seahorse), and sw-fish-044 (Ingen's Seahorse).

Dragonface Pipefish is strongly advised to be kept in a species-only or pipefish-and-seahorse-only aquarium because faster-moving tankmates outcompete it for food leading to starvation; a typed boolean (analogous to crustacean.speciesOnlyTankRecommended) would surface this critical compatibility constraint. Fifth independent proposal after sw-fish-004, sw-fish-020, sw-fish-053, and sw-fish-044.

Chaetodon falcula forms monogamous pair bonds in the wild and is commonly recommended to be kept as a mated pair in captivity (consistent with C. semilarvatus and C. pelewensis proposals); a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface pair-bonding acquisition guidance to aquarists. Fourth independent proposal after sw-fish-034, sw-fish-032, and sw-fish-053.

Naso elegans, like all Acanthuridae (surgeonfish and unicornfish), possesses paired permanently-erect caudal-peduncle keels (scalpels) that can injure handlers and tangle nets; a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface a handling-safety warning distinct from venomousSpines. Sixth independent proposal after sw-fish-018, sw-fish-023, sw-fish-013, sw-fish-066, and sw-fish-076.

Naso elegans juveniles are gray with yellow dorsal and anal fin stripes, transforming into the tan-and-orange adult coloration over several years — a change pronounced enough that juveniles may not be recognized as the same species in the trade; a typed boolean would surface this to buyers. Fourth independent proposal after sw-fish-048 (Koran Angelfish), sw-fish-056 (Maculosus Angelfish), and sw-fish-047 (Jewel Damselfish, colorChangesWithAge variant).

Cirrhilabrus punctatus and other fairy wrasses form harems in the wild (one terminal male with multiple females); keeping one male with several females is the recommended captive approach — a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface harem-keeping and protogynous hermaphroditism guidance to fishkeepers. Fifth independent proposal after sw-fish-005 (Flame Angelfish), sw-fish-049 (Lamarck Angelfish), sw-fish-039 (Half-Black Angelfish), and sw-fish-093 (Bluefin Angelfish).

Wolf Eel (Anarrhichthys ocellatus) reaches 240 cm and requires cold North Pacific water (1–10°C) incompatible with typical tropical home aquariums; multiple sources explicitly categorize it as suitable for large public aquarium or zoo display only — a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface this practicality warning to home aquarists researching the species. Second independent proposal after sw-fish-036 (Threadfin Lookdown).

Wolf Eel forms documented lifelong monogamous pair bonds — both sexes cohabit the same den for decades and cooperate in egg guarding; a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface pair-bonding acquisition and housing guidance to aquarists. Fifth independent proposal after sw-fish-034, sw-fish-032, sw-fish-053, and sw-070.

Cubicus Boxfish (and all Ostraciidae) can release ostracitoxin from mucous glands when stressed or dying, rapidly killing all tankmates in a closed system — a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface this critical safety warning in the UI, distinct from venomousSpines (handler-injected venom) and aggression. Third independent proposal after sw-fish-055 (Longhorn Cowfish) and sw-fish-069 (Arothron Puffer).

Cubicus Boxfish juveniles are vivid yellow with brown spots; adults fade to brownish coloration with yellow-spotted patterning — a change striking enough that juveniles and adults look like different species in vendor photos; a typed boolean would allow the UI to warn buyers about the transformation. Fifth independent proposal after sw-fish-048, sw-fish-056, sw-fish-085, and sw-fish-079.

Fijian Mimic Lemon Peel Tang, like other Acanthurus surgeonfish, is prone to Head and Lateral Line Erosion (HLLE) when dietary diversity or water quality is inadequate; a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface a disease-risk warning and diet/water-quality guidance for HLLE-susceptible species. Fourth independent proposal after sw-fish-015 (Yellow Tang), sw-fish-066 (Sohal Tang), and sw-fish-076 (Black-Eared Tang).

Acanthurus pyroferus undergoes a dramatic ontogenetic color change: juveniles are vivid yellow with blue eye highlights (mimicking Centropyge flavissimus), while adults become tan/brown with red highlights and green-tinged fins — so pronounced that juveniles and adults are often not recognized as the same fish in the trade. Fourth independent proposal after sw-fish-047 (Jewel Damselfish), sw-fish-078 (Blackmouth Bicolor Chromis), and sw-fish-091 (Blue Velvet Damselfish).

Gymnothorax miliaris is documented by FishBase as occasionally hunting cooperatively with other species (e.g. groupers) — an unusual behavior for moray eels that would be useful to surface in a typed boolean for species-filter UI and keeper education.

Apolemichthys trimaculatus forms small harems of one dominant male and 4–5 females in the wild (Monaco Nature Encyclopedia); a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface harem-keeping and protogynous hermaphroditism guidance to fishkeepers. Sixth independent proposal after sw-fish-005, sw-fish-049, sw-fish-039, sw-fish-093, and sw-fish-109.

Bluestripe Pipefish (Doryrhamphus excisus) use their prehensile tails to anchor onto gorgonians, coral branches, and macroalgae; a typed boolean on the fish variant would surface this non-obvious tank-setup requirement to fishkeepers distinctly from general decorPreferences. Fifth independent proposal after sw-fish-004 (Estuary Seahorse), sw-fish-053 (Lined Seahorse), sw-fish-044 (Ingen's Seahorse), and sw-fish-103 (Dragonface Pipefish).

Bluestripe Pipefish are slow feeders easily outcompeted by faster-moving fish; sources consistently advise housing in a species-only or pipefish/seahorse-only aquarium to prevent starvation — a typed boolean (analogous to crustacean.speciesOnlyTankRecommended) would surface this critical compatibility constraint. Sixth independent proposal after sw-fish-004, sw-fish-020, sw-fish-053, sw-fish-044, and sw-fish-103.
